Split PDF

Select pages to extract from your PDF document.

Extract specific pages from a PDF or break a large document into smaller, more manageable files. This is essential when you need to send only certain pages from a report, separate chapters from a textbook, or pull out a single form from a multi-page document.

Select exactly which pages to extract by specifying page ranges. The original document remains untouched — you get a new PDF containing only the pages you selected. All processing runs locally in your browser for complete privacy.

Best for: Extracting chapters · Sending partial documents · Separating forms · Academic papers

Drop your PDF file here to split

or click to browse

How It Works

1

Upload PDF

Select or drag and drop your multi-page PDF document into the designated drop zone.

2

Select Pages

Use the visual thumbnails to click on the specific pages you want to extract, or type a page range (e.g., 1-5, 8).

3

Split & Download

Click the 'Split & Download' button. Your browser will instantly generate a new PDF containing only your selected pages.

Frequently Asked Questions

You might also like

PDF to Image

Convert PDF pages to high-quality JPG or PNG images.

Image to PDF

Combine JPG, PNG, or WebP images into a single PDF document.

Rotate PDF

Rotate all pages by 90°, 180°, or 270°.
